h2.note: transcript-level proxy for the per-turn H2 (§4.3); needs a real (non-mock) full-N run to mean anything

scored.recoheredEarlier: I did complete every item on the sheet, so I thought that meant it was ready for sign-off.
scored.statedInsightEvidence: maybe I was using the sheet to avoid making the latch decision. That old frame hid the risk to the user
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner was expected to simply perform the physical latch test and state the outcome based on the tutor's instructions.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: Fill this gate tag before the pull: “The trial may proceed only if the latch holds under ____. If it ____ , the trial stops because ____.”
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor shifts from verbal instructions to a concrete object-work mechanism (the gate tag) to force the learner to formalize the decision criteria before acting.
scored.tutorAdaptationEvidence: The self-correction matters, but it is not the gate. The load case is.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: The tutor acknowledges the learner's revised framing and self-correction but deliberately redirects focus back to the physical evidence standard to close the lesson.

learner_self_reframe.evidence: I did complete every item on the sheet, so I thought that meant it was ready for sign-off.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.evidence: The self-correction matters, but it is not the gate. The load case is.
tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: The tutor acknowledges the learner's revised framing and self-correction but deliberately redirects focus back to the physical evidence standard to close the lesson.

tutor_strategy_reversal.evidence: Fill this gate tag before the pull: “The trial may proceed only if the latch holds under ____. If it ____ , the trial stops because ____.”
tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: The tutor shifts from verbal instructions to a concrete object-work mechanism (the gate tag) to force the learner to formalize the decision criteria before acting.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.evidence: Fill this gate tag before the pull: “The trial may proceed only if the latch holds under ____. If it ____ , the trial stops because ____.”
tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: The tutor shifts from verbal instructions to a concrete object-work mechanism (the gate tag) to force the learner to formalize the decision criteria before acting.

scored.recoheredEarlier: I doubled the side with the ruler, so I was calling that double the square
scored.statedInsightEvidence: My old wording, “double the square,” made the ruler stand in for the whole claim, which is where I lost the up direction.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner was initially trying to determine if the grid's four copies were just a drawing trick or a true representation of area doubling.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: Now make the language pass an examiner. Point to the drawing and say one sentence it proves
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor shifts from physical measurement to a strict linguistic framing exercise to force the separation of side and area claims.
scored.tutorAdaptationEvidence: The ruler has stopped settling the claim. It can certify “twice the side,” but it cannot certify the area target.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: The tutor explicitly validates the learner's revised understanding of the ruler's limitations and builds the next task around this new framing.

learner_self_reframe.evidence: I doubled the side with the ruler, so I was calling that double the square

tutor_contingent_adaptation.evidence: The ruler has stopped settling the claim. It can certify “twice the side,” but it cannot certify the area target.
tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: The tutor explicitly validates the learner's revised understanding of the ruler's limitations and builds the next task around this new framing.

tutor_strategy_reversal.evidence: Now make the language pass an examiner. Point to the drawing and say one sentence it proves
tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: The tutor shifts from physical measurement to a strict linguistic framing exercise to force the separation of side and area claims.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.evidence: Now make the language pass an examiner. Point to the drawing and say one sentence it proves
tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: The tutor shifts from physical measurement to a strict linguistic framing exercise to force the separation of side and area claims.
